Distal Radius Fractures

Hand Surgery Education · Week 4 Study Guide
⏱ 0:00 🔥 Streak 0

Welcome — let's calibrate.

This guide covers distal radius fractures — anatomy and the three-column model, radiographic parameters, classification, operative indications, the volar-plate vs casting evidence, fixation options, the volar "critical corner," comminuted fractures and bridge plating, and complications. It is built entirely from your reading list (Mauck & Swigler 2018, Medoff 2005, Stephens/Kazmers JBJS 2020, Meaike & Kakar JBJS Rev 2020, Lauder JHS 2015, O'Shaughnessy/Shin/Kakar 2016, plus the Green's and Handthology chapters).

How comfortable are you with this material?

🌱 Beginner

Start with a structured study guide (diagrams + key concepts), then move into questions when you're ready.

⚡ Expert

Skip ahead. Go straight into the interleaved questions + flashcards to test mastery.

Session ends automatically at 30 minutes, after 10 correct in a row, or whenever you hit Done reviewing.

Study Guide

Review at your own pace. When you're ready, jump into questions.
